signals.title: USD/JPY — G8 Bilateral Theme + Yield Dis
signals.text: JPY 1W avg +1.081% (n=7) vs USD -0.874% (n=7) sets up a strong bilateral theme; however, the 196bp US-JGB yield gap reinforces USD/JPY upside — crowded JPY short -88.5k creates carry extension. The yield disadvantage for JPY is likely to limit its upside, with significant JPY short positioning creating a critical setup for the next session. G8 spread: 1.955%

signals.title: EUR/USD — Tariff Escalation and ECB Hold
signals.text: Trump's tariff escalation on European autos sets up downside pressure on EUR/USD toward 1.16; EUR COT short -18.3k reinforces the bias. The ECB's Hold stance at 2.00% and next meeting on 30 Apr may further influence the pair's direction, given the US-EU trade tensions. The mechanism driving the move is the potential decrease in European auto exports to the US, affecting the euro's value.
evidence: Trump's tariff escalation on European autos

signals.title: AUD/USD — RBA Hike Bias and Crowded Long
signals.text: AUD COT LF long +48.3k (74% long) creates crowded-long unwind risk — AUD/USD closed 0.7208 Friday; RBA Hike bias (0% priced) sustains the bid but any disappointment triggers the unwind. The carry advantage of +35bp for AUD over USD is likely to support the pair, but the crowded long positioning poses a significant risk. Next RBA meeting is on 5 May.

signals.title: GBP/USD — BoE Hold and UK-Iran Tensions
signals.text: GBP/USD closed Friday at 1.3575; BoE's Hold at 3.75% and next meeting on 30 Apr may keep the pair stable, but UK-Iran tensions and the US-Iran peace talks could introduce volatility. The mechanism driving the move is the potential impact of geopolitical events on the pound's value, considering the BoE's Hold stance.

signals.title: USD/CAD — Fed Hold and Oil Price Impact
signals.text: USD/CAD closed Friday at 1.3586; the Fed's Hold at 3.75% and next meeting on 29 Apr, combined with the recent drop in WTI oil prices, is likely to influence the pair's direction. The mechanism driving the move is the potential decrease in oil exports' value to the US, affecting the Canadian dollar's value.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: With NZD/USD at 0.5898, the -150bp carry differential favors the USD, but specs are short -16.8k contracts, positioning themselves against the RBNZ's forward bias for a hike, with 40% of a hike priced in. This sets up a crowded-short unwind risk, as the market is likely to unwind its positioning if the RBNZ signals a more hawkish tone, creating an asymmetric risk. The next RBNZ meeting on 27 May will be a key catalyst.
